title: prunus lloydii
intro: Prunus lloydii, also known as the Lloyd's cherry or mountain cherry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae) native to the Himalayas. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly the bark, which contains tannins and other compounds that have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Prunus lloydii include reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Prunus lloydii can be prepared as a decoction, a tea made by infusing the bark in hot water, which is often used in traditional medicine in the Himalayan region.
